name: ISO/PAS 8800: Road vehicles — Safety and artificial intelligence
disclaimer: Informative only. Not a substitute for reading the published standard.

entries.title: AI safety lifecycle overview
entries.summary: Defines the overall lifecycle for managing safety of AI-based systems in road vehicles, including planning, development, deployment, and decommissioning phases.

entries.title: General principles for AI safety
entries.summary: Establishes foundational principles for ensuring AI components in vehicles meet safety objectives throughout their lifecycle.

entries.title: AI safety plan
entries.summary: Requires creation and maintenance of a safety plan that addresses AI-specific risks, responsibilities, and milestones for the development project.

entries.title: Integration with ISO 26262
entries.summary: Describes how AI safety activities integrate with the functional safety lifecycle defined in ISO 26262, including ASIL-based tailoring.

entries.title: Integration with ISO 21448 (SOTIF)
entries.summary: Explains the relationship between AI safety management and SOTIF processes for handling functional insufficiencies.

entries.title: AI safety requirements
entries.summary: Covers derivation and specification of safety requirements that are specific to AI components in road vehicles.

entries.title: Hazard analysis for AI components
entries.summary: Requires systematic identification of hazards that may arise from AI-specific failure modes such as misclassification, distributional shift, or adversarial inputs.

entries.title: AI safety requirements specification
entries.summary: Defines how to derive safety requirements for AI elements from hazard analysis, including performance boundaries and failure detection requirements.

entries.title: Functional performance requirements
entries.summary: Specifies measurable functional performance criteria that the AI component must satisfy under defined operational conditions.

entries.title: Robustness requirements
entries.summary: Addresses requirements for AI model resilience to input variations, noise, and edge cases encountered in vehicle operation.

entries.title: Operational design domain specification
entries.summary: Requires clear specification of the operational design domain within which the AI system is intended to function safely.

entries.title: Data quality and management
entries.summary: Addresses the role of data quality in achieving AI safety, covering data collection, annotation, curation, and lifecycle management.

entries.title: Data quality objectives
entries.summary: Requires defining measurable data quality objectives including relevance, completeness, balance, and accuracy of training and validation datasets.

entries.title: Data collection and sourcing
entries.summary: Provides guidance on collecting data that is representative of the operational design domain, including real-world and synthetic data sources.

entries.title: Data annotation and labelling
entries.summary: Specifies requirements for annotation processes including inter-annotator agreement, label quality, and handling of ambiguous cases.

entries.title: Data sufficiency and coverage
entries.summary: Requires demonstration that datasets provide sufficient coverage of the operational domain, including corner cases and rare scenarios.

entries.title: Data versioning and traceability
entries.summary: Mandates version control and traceability for all datasets used in training, validation, and testing of AI models.

entries.title: Bias identification and mitigation
entries.summary: Addresses identification and mitigation of biases in training data that could lead to unsafe AI behavior in certain conditions.

entries.title: Model development and verification
entries.summary: Covers the AI model design, training, and verification activities needed to achieve safety objectives.

entries.title: Model architecture selection
entries.summary: Provides guidance on selecting AI model architectures considering safety, interpretability, and the ability to verify behavior.

entries.title: Training process control
entries.summary: Requires documented control of the training process including hyperparameter selection, convergence monitoring, and reproducibility.

entries.title: Model verification methods
entries.summary: Specifies verification activities for AI models including testing against requirements, formal analysis where applicable, and inspection of learned behavior.

entries.title: Test-based verification
entries.summary: Defines requirements for test-based verification of AI models, including test coverage metrics, test case generation, and pass/fail criteria.

entries.title: Analysis-based verification
entries.summary: Addresses analytical methods for verifying AI model properties such as sensitivity analysis, neuron coverage analysis, and formal verification where feasible.

entries.title: Model interpretability and explainability
entries.summary: Recommends methods to understand and explain AI model decisions to support safety assessment and debugging.

entries.title: Model robustness evaluation
entries.summary: Requires evaluation of model robustness against adversarial perturbations, domain shift, and out-of-distribution inputs.

entries.title: Validation of AI-based systems
entries.summary: Covers validation activities to confirm that the AI system meets its intended safety goals at the vehicle level.

entries.title: Validation strategy and planning
entries.summary: Requires a documented validation strategy that defines scope, methods, acceptance criteria, and the relationship between simulation, track, and field testing.

entries.title: Scenario-based validation
entries.summary: Specifies the use of scenario-based approaches for validating AI safety, including logical and concrete scenario derivation.

entries.title: Simulation-based validation
entries.summary: Addresses the use of simulation environments for safety validation, including simulation fidelity, coverage, and credibility assessment.

entries.title: Residual risk evaluation
entries.summary: Requires evaluation of residual safety risk from the AI component after verification and validation activities are complete.

entries.title: Operational phase and monitoring
entries.summary: Addresses activities during the operational phase of AI systems in vehicles, including monitoring, updating, and field feedback.

entries.title: Operational monitoring strategy
entries.summary: Requires defining a strategy for monitoring AI system performance during field operation, including degradation detection and alert mechanisms.

entries.title: Performance drift detection
entries.summary: Specifies methods for detecting performance drift of deployed AI models due to changes in operating environment or data distribution.

entries.title: Field data collection and feedback
entries.summary: Requires processes for collecting field data to support continuous improvement, incident analysis, and model retraining decisions.

entries.title: Model update and change management
entries.summary: Addresses the process for safely updating AI models in the field, including impact analysis, re-verification, and rollback procedures.

entries.title: Decommissioning considerations
entries.summary: Provides guidance on safe decommissioning of AI-based vehicle systems, including data retention and transition planning.
